method: Fractional differentiation (Lopez de Prado ch.5): fixed-width-window fracdiff of log-price at minimum-d (ADF) + ridge/mean-rev + overlay.
libs_implemented: fixed-width-window fractional-diff binomial weights (numpy); Augmented Dickey-Fuller t-stat with AIC lag selection (numpy); closed-form ridge regression (numpy). No pywt/statsmodels used.
notes: LdP fixed-width fracdiff (ADF t-stat + binomial weights impl. in numpy). Min-d (IS log-price, 5% crit -2.86) mean d*=0.15 across 14 perps (8h, 4890 bars). KEY TEST: fractional vs integer-diff(d=1 returns) in a pooled-ridge cross-sec market-neutral book: FRAC_only-RET_only OOS Sharpe=-1.18, BOTH-RET_only=-1.19 (fractional part adds NO value). Best standalone=pred:RET_only OOS Sharpe=-0.88 ret=-24.1%/yr PSR=0.15; mean-rev(fade z) OOS Sharpe=-2.38. OVERLAY (|fracdiff z| gate on long-BTC): base Sharpe=0.10 -> gated 0.16, maxDD -55%->-44% (helps=True). DEFLATION: 45 variants -> within-family DSR SR*=0.0248, best OOS SR_pp=-0.0266 (fails), deflated-PSR=0.02. CRITIQUE: fracdiff is a high-knob method (d,tau,z_win,ridge_lam,thresholds); any standalone Sharpe must clear the inflated DSR bar AND beat the d=1 baseline to credit the FRACTIONAL part specifically. Directional/mean-rev priors died here, so a stationary-but-memory-rich series alone is unlikely to resurrect them.
data_caveats: close-to-close 8h perp closes; d* frozen from IS log-price (no leak); fracdiff/ADF/ridge fit on IS only; market-neutral close-to-close maxDD understates intrabar liq/gap; adf_per_coin={"BTCUSDT": 0.3, "ETHUSDT": 0.15, "BNBUSDT": 0.2, "SOLUSDT": 0.3, "XRPUSDT": 0.0, "ADAUSDT": 0.05, "DOGEUSDT": 0.05, "LTCUSDT": 0.0, "LINKUSDT": 0.15, "AVAXUSDT": 0.2, "TRXUSDT": 0.25, "DOTUSDT": 0.05, "NEARUSDT": 0.25, "ATOMUSDT": 0.2}
